title: Finite groups with a nilpotent maximal subgroup
journal: Journal of the Australian Mathematical Society
abstract: Let G be a finite group all of whose proper subgroups are nilpotent. Then by a theorem of Schmidt-Iwasawa the group G is soluble. But what can we say about a finite group G is only one maximal subgroup is nilpotent? Let G be a finite group with a nilpotent maximal subgroup M.
